cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question step by step. The question is about what we know about garage sales based on the provided passage. The options are A to D, and I need to pick the correct one. First, let me recall the passage. The passage discusses both flea markets and garage sales. The part about garage sales starts with "Perhaps even more enjoyable than flea markets are the weekend outdoor garage sales which have now become very much a part of American culture." So right here, it's saying that garage sales are a part of American culture, which implies they are popular. That might relate to option C. Looking at the options again: A) It is only held in the spring of the year. The passage says "usually in the spring and fall when the weather is still good." The word "usually" doesn't mean exclusively, so this might not be correct. The question is about what we know for sure. The answer can't be A because it says "only" spring, but the passage mentions both spring and fall. B) The goods on display are totally useless. The passage says that the homeowner wants to clear out items that are "no longer useful to him." However, the next sentence says "More than likely the goods on display are in good condition and great buys can be found..." So the items are not necessarily useless; they might be in good condition. So B is incorrect. C) Garage sales are popular with Americans. The first sentence about garage sales says they "have now become very much a part of American culture."
